title: General Addictions/Substance Use Disorder Support Groups - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Mental & Behavioral Health > General Addictions/Substance Use Disorder Support Groups 21 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - No Filter African American Community No Filter 4TH DIMENSION RECOVERY CENTER CLACKAMAS CENTER ADDICTION RECOVERY PEER MENTORING SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers peer mentoring services for substance use disorders. Mentors help clients to find housing, employment, applying for benefits such as TANF and OHP, guidance about legal aid programs, and transportation to recovery-oriented meetings and events. Also offers 12-step meetings and recovery-related activities and events, including dances, barbecues, poetry nights, and movie nights. 11690 SE 82nd Avenue Happy Valley, OR 97086 Eligibility: Individuals age 14-35 with substance use issues. Clients can be currently in treatment, just leaving treatment, or at any other stage of their recovery process. Hours: 7 days per week 2pm-10pm Email (971) 703-4623 Get Directions Visit Website More Details 4TH DIMENSION RECOVERY CENTER O'ROURKE CENTER ADDICTION RECOVERY PEER MENTORING SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers peer mentoring services for substance use disorders. Mentors help clients to find housing, employment, applying for benefits such as TANF and OHP, guidance about legal aid programs, and transportation to recovery-oriented meetings and events. Also offers 12-step meetings and recovery-related activities and events, including dances, barbecues, poetry nights, and movie nights. 362 SW Oak Street Hillsboro, OR 97123 Eligibility: Individuals age 14-35 with substance use issues. Clients can be currently in treatment, just leaving treatment, or at any other stage of their recovery process. Hours: 7 days per week 2pm-10pm Email (971) 703-4623 Get Directions Visit Website More Details 4TH DIMENSION RECOVERY CENTER SMIT CENTER ADDICTION RECOVERY PEER MENTORING SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers peer mentoring services for substance use disorders. Mentors help clients to find housing, employment, applying for benefits such as TANF and OHP, guidance about legal aid programs, and transportation to recovery-oriented meetings and events. Also offers 12-step meetings and recovery-related activities and events, including dances, barbecues, poetry nights, and movie nights. 324 NE 9th Street Gresham, OR 97030 Eligibility: Individuals age 14-35 with substance use issues. Clients can be currently in treatment, just leaving treatment, or at any other stage of their recovery process. Hours: 7 days per week 2pm-10pm Email (971) 703-4623 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BAY AREA FIRST STEP INC RESOURCE DEPARTMENT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a drop in space for social activities. Offers assistance with resume building, help to quit smoking, and access to support groups for substance use issues and parenting. 155 S Empire Boulevard Coos Bay, OR 97420 Eligibility: Individuals and families with minor children in substance use disorder recovery Hours: Monday/Thursday 8am-8pm, Tuesday-Wednesday/Friday 8am-5pm, Saturday 1pm-4pm, Sunday 8am-4:30pm Email (541) 756-3111 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BOISE RESCUE MISSION MINISTRIES CITY LIGHT HOME FOR WOMEN AND CHILDREN NEW LIFE DISCIPLESHIP AND RECOVERY PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Twelve to twenty-four month addiction recovery program that provides discipleship, accountability, and a Bible-based curriculum designed to help participants live sober. 1404 W Jefferson Street Boise, ID 83702 Eligibility: Women experiencing a substance use disorder. Children may accompany their mothers. Hours: Hours vary Email (208) 368-9901 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BOISE RESCUE MISSION MINISTRIES RIVER OF LIFE MEN'S SHELTER NEW LIFE DISCIPLESHIP AND RECOVERY PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Twelve to twenty-four month addiction recovery program that provides discipleship, accountability, and a Bible-based curriculum designed to help participants live sober. 575 S 13th Street Boise, ID 83702 Eligibility: Men experiencing a substance use disorder. Hours: Hours vary Email Get Directions Visit Website More Details CELEBRATE RECOVERY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a faith-based 12-step recovery program and online search tool to find groups to attend. 1 Saddleback Parkway Lake Forest, CA 92630 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Not applicable Email (877) 727-8677 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FREE ON THE OUTSIDE COMMUNITY MEAL Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a community meal twice a week followed by a church service or Celebrate Recovery, 12-step meeting, in which attendance is not required to obtain a meal. 529 Molalla Avenue Oregon City, OR 97045 Eligibility: Age 18 and older Hours: Meal: Thursday 5:15pm, Sunday 5pm Celebrate Recovery: Thursday 6:30pm Worship Service: Sunday 6pm Email (503) 383-1834 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LIFEWORKS NW LIFEWORKS NW PROJECT NETWORK PROJECT NETWORK Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides substance use disorder support groups, counseling, and intervention programs. Daycare during programs is available. Eligibility: Women age 18 and older and women with children ages 0-8 Hours: Varies Email (503) 335-0855 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OPTIONS FOR SOUTHERN OREGON INC THE HUB Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a recovery-oriented drop-in center where participants can sit, have a snack, and connect with others. Services include case management and information and referral to community resources, job finding assistance and employment support. Offers a variety of support groups that cover topics such as wellness, Medication Assisted Treatment (MAT) Education, and making connections. Assist with low-barrier access to treatment services and provides peer support for substance-related issues. Substance use and mental health assessments can be done by telehealth. Provides referrals for MAT. Distributes naloxone and offers education in partnership with other agencies. 404 SW 5th Street Grants Pass, OR 97526 Eligibility: Age 18 and older Hours: Monday/Tuesday/Thursday/Friday 9am-5pm, Wednesday 9am-12:30pm and 3pm-5pm (541) 916-5723 Get Directions Visit Website More Details PEERGALAXY PEERGALAXY ONLINE COMMUNITY MEETINGS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides online community meetings to discuss various positive topics. These topics include life skills, self-reliance, creative expression, spiritual growth, wellness, peer support, and healing and recovery from mental health challenges, addictions, trauma, and grief. Meetings occur weekly and/or seasonally on the HeyPeers platform, usually on most days. Each meeting series typically lasts 10 weeks, although some may be longer or shorter, and each session generally lasts for 1 hour. Website Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Varies Email Visit Website More Details PROVOKING HOPE RECOVERY SUPPORT SERVICES ADDICTION RECOVERY SUPPORT SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides peer, one-on-one, and group support on topics such as recovery, relationships, stress, co-dependency, parenting, and grief. Also provides specialized information and referrals. 213 NE 10th Street McMinnville, OR 97128 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-noon and 1pm-5pm Email (971) 228-2020 Get Directions Visit Website More Details RECLAIMING LIVES RECOVERY CAFÉ Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides recovery circles for individuals with alcohol and substances use disorders. Services include peer support mentoring, various classes including relapse prevention, meals and espresso-style coffee when attending the recovery circles, and family member support. 228 East Main Street Suite H Medford, OR 97501 Eligibility: Recovery Café: Age 18 and older who are 24 hours clean and sober Peer services and relapse prevention classes: Age 18 and older Hours: Recovery Café: Sunday 4pm-6:30pm, Monday-Tuesday 5pm-7:30pm El Camino Seguro: Thursday 5pm-7:30pm Educational classes: 5 days per week, times vary Peer support and office hours: Monday-Thursday 9am-5pm Email (541) 326-1233 Get Directions Visit Website More Details RECOVERY CAFE ROSEWAY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a place for recovery peer support and connections through recovery circles, community meals, an espresso bar, and various calendar events. Peer support hours may include help accessing health, employment, housing, and treatment services. 3516 NE 71st Avenue Portland, OR 97213 Eligibility: Membership based which requires: -Drug and alcohol free when entering the cafe community space -Attend a weekly Recovery Circle -Contribute to the cafe community through acts of service Hours: Tuesday-Thursday 11am-4pm, lunch 12:15pm-1:30pm Email (971) 459-9899 Get Directions Visit Website More Details RECOVERY OUTREACH COMMUNITY CENTER SALEM PEER SUPPORT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers peer support for mental health, addiction, trauma and stress. Provides one-on-one support, support groups, and phones that are available seven days a week for texting or calling. There are eighteen different groups per week. 1190 Broadway Street NE Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Ages 18 and older Hours: Monday/Tuesday/Wednesday/Saturday/Sunday 11am-4pm Email (971) 332-5676 Get Directions Visit Website More Details RECOVERY OUTREACH COMMUNITY CENTER SOCC PEER SUPPORT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers peer support for mental health, addiction, trauma and stress. Provides one-on-one support, support groups, and phones that are available seven days a week for texting or calling. There are eighteen different groups per week. 280 NE Santiam Boulevard Mill City, OR 97360 Eligibility: Ages 18 and older Hours: Monday/Tuesday/Wednesday/Saturday/Sunday 11am-4pm Email (971) 732-8668 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ROLLING HILLS COMMUNITY CHURCH CELEBRATE RECOVERY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides small group support for drug and alcohol addictions, sexual integrity, mental health concerns, codependency, anger, anxiety, depression, and various types of abuse. 3550 SW Borland Road Tualatin, OR 97062 Eligibility: Individuals in recovery or others needing coping support Hours: Thursday 6pm-8:30pm; optional dinner at 5:30pm Email (503) 638-5900 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SMART RECOVERY USA ADDICTION SUPPORT GROUPS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides support groups and peer counseling to help individuals gain independence from addiction and addictive behaviors, including substance use disorder, gambling addiction, and sexual addiction. Offers a "no higher power" approach. The program has four aspects: building motivation, coping with urges, problem-solving, and developing a balanced lifestyle. 7304 Mentor Avenue Suite F Mentor, OH 44060 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Varies by meeting Email (440) 951-5357 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SOARING HEIGHTS RECOVERY HOMES PEER SUPPORT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides peer support services. Eligibility: Individuals who self-identify as having a substance use disorder Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-8pm Email (971) 719-4963 Get Directions Visit Website More Details TILLAMOOK SERENITY CLUB SUBSTANCE USE DISORDER SUPPORT GROUPS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides peer support and a meeting space for all 12-step recovery groups. Opioid overdose supplies are available to the public in a weatherproof box located outside the building. 5012 3rd Street Tillamook, OR 97141 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Meetings: Varies Drop in: Tuesday-Friday 11am-7:30pm Opioid overdose kit: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (503) 842-1115 Get Directions Visit Website More Details WHITE BIRD CLINIC WHITE BIRD CHRYSALIS CHRYSALIS BEHAVIORAL HEALTH OUTPATIENT SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides outpatient substance abuse services and treatment. Also offers support groups, drug education classes, couples and individual counseling, peer support, acupuncture, yoga, tai chi, parenting groups, women's groups, opiate primary group, medical reviews, drugs screening, after care, and DUII services that meet state requirements. 350 E 11th Avenue Eugene, OR 97401 Eligibility: Age 18 and older Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-6pm Email (541) 683-1641 Get Directions Visit Website More Details 4TH DIMENSION RECOVERY CENTER ADDICTION RECOVERY PEER MENTORING SERVICES 4TH DIMENSION RECOVERY CENTER ADDICTION RECOVERY PEER MENTORING SERVICES 4TH DIMENSION RECOVERY CENTER ADDICTION RECOVERY PEER MENTORING SERVICES BAY AREA FIRST STEP INC RESOURCE DEPARTMENT FREE ON THE OUTSIDE COMMUNITY MEAL OPTIONS FOR SOUTHERN OREGON INC THE HUB PROVOKING HOPE RECOVERY SUPPORT SERVICES ADDICTION RECOVERY SUPPORT SERVICES RECLAIMING LIVES RECOVERY CAFÉ RECOVERY CAFE ROSEWAY RECOVERY CAFE ROSEWAY RECOVERY OUTREACH COMMUNITY CENTER PEER SUPPORT RECOVERY OUTREACH COMMUNITY CENTER PEER SUPPORT ROLLING HILLS COMMUNITY CHURCH CELEBRATE RECOVERY TILLAMOOK SERENITY CLUB SUBSTANCE USE DISORDER SUPPORT GROUPS WHITE BIRD CLINIC CHRYSALIS BEHAVIORAL HEALTH OUTPATIENT SERVICES
